summary: Set 023 Session 3: Codebase-wide audit of events-ledger / progress-state consumers, added to Set 023 after Session 2's design review surfaced the third sharp edge from both providers (Gemini: critical; GPT: major). Mechanical grep sweep across Python (ai_router/) and TypeScript (tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/) for events-ledger / closeout_succeeded / read_events references, cross-referenced against completedSessions references. 18 consumers classified: 11 already-correct (5 gate predicates + Set 022 helpers + TS reader paths); 7 correct-to-ignore (reconciler stranded-state sweep, lifecycle derivation, events-ledger I/O helpers, backfill, file watcher, close_session._is_already_closed with note, tooltip prose); 1 newly-surfaced Python sharp edge fixed in-session (ai_router.print_session_set_status's pre-Set-022 activity-log distinct-sessionNumber count, the same shape Set 022 Session 2 removed from the TypeScript reader); 1 known sharp edge already in Session 4's scope (TypeScript isMidSetComplete, listed for completeness). Fix: replaced the activity-log derivation with len(compute_effective_completed_sessions(path)), the Set 022 single-source-of-truth helper. Tests: 4 regression fixtures (in-flight count, legacy fallback, events-ledger fallback per round-1 verifier finding, done-set array length); 720 tests pass total. Borderline classification of close_session._is_already_closed defaulted to correct-to-ignore-with-note per the operator's 'lowest-engagement bucket' preference and the function's docstring rationale; documented for re-evaluation if a real incident surfaces it. Released as ai_router 0.2.5 via tag-driven workflow. TypeScript sharp edges deferred to Session 4's v0.13.13 bundle. Cross-provider verification (gpt-5-4, session-verification) round 1 ISSUES_FOUND: 2 Minor — overstated 'resolved on disk' claim (fixed: reworded to distinguish 'audit performed' from 'systemic concern fully eliminated which awaits Session 4'); missing events-ledger fallback test (fixed: added test_events_ledger_fallback_when_array_absent). No round-2 reroute: both findings textually verifiable; recommended_fix exactly matches what was applied. Session 3 routed spend: $0.147. Cumulative Set 023 routed spend through Session 3: $0.544.

reason.specifics: Session 4 is the originally-planned reader-side fix for the TypeScript extension's isMidSetComplete guard, plus the audit-driven refinements both providers surfaced in Session 2 (observability warn when array overrides missing ledger closeout, sharpened authoritative-doc language, fixtures F5/F6/F7 for non-array completedSessions / stray out-of-range entries / non-final-session disagreements). The Session 3 audit-findings document confirmed no additional TypeScript sharp edges need to bundle into v0.13.13 — only the reader fix and the audit refinements. Same Claude that just authored Session 3's audit has the freshest context on what classifies as a TypeScript sharp edge under the Set 022 invariant and what the audit deliberately left correct-to-ignore. The change surface is small (a five-line addition to fileSystem.ts plus the audit-driven fixtures and the observability warn) and the test suite is well-scoped. Effort-low stands.
